companies.name: French observability startup Tsuga
companies.blurb: Tsuga, a Paris-based innovator in AI-Native Resilient Observability, today announced it has raised €30 million ($35 million) in a Series A funding round to scale its GTM motion and accelerate the rollout of their AI agent platform. The round is led by existing investor Singular, with participation from General Catalyst alongside new investors DST Global […] The post French observability startup Tsuga lands €30 million to expand AI agent platform appeared first on EU-Startups .

companies.name: Bristol’s Astral Systems
companies.blurb: Astral Systems, a British DeepTech firm pioneering multi-state fusion (MSF) technology, has secured €26 million (£23 million) in Series A funding in order to bring critical medical isotopes to market by early 2027, scaling the production of its modular MSF reactors, and advancing fusion research. This first close investment was led by Mercia Ventures and […] The post Bristol’s Astral Systems secures €26 million Series A to support medical isotope commercialisation appeared first on EU-Startups .

companies.name: 14 million poorly insulated German homes: VARM
companies.blurb: VARM, a Berlin-based startup training and deploying insulation specialists, has raised €17.5 million in a Series A funding round to expand its scalable home insulation model across Germany and, eventually, Europe. The round was led by ABN AMRO Sustainable Impact Fund and co-led by GET Fund, with participation from Aurum Impact and existing investors Emerge […] The post 14 million poorly insulated German homes: VARM raises €17.5 million to expand its installer network appeared first on EU-Startups .

companies.name: After betting the firm on Anthropic, Menlo Ventures
companies.blurb: Menlo has created a solid rep for itself as an AI investor, all based on one gutsy $750 million move in 2024.

companies.name: Seedcamp
companies.blurb: After 18 years of focusing on Europe, early-stage investor Seedcamp said that it has raised $320 million for its latest fund, which will see it expanding its presence in the United States.

companies.name: Catalio Capital
companies.blurb: Catalio Capital Management announced the closing of its more than $400 million Fund IV. The fund will continue the firm’s thesis of backing healthcare and biotechnology companies.

companies.name: Unmanned cargo aircraft developer Acodyne
companies.blurb: Copenhagen-based DeepTech startup Acodyne has raised €2.5 million in pre-Seed funding to scale its unmanned cargo aircraft for heavy-lift logistics in defence, offshore and remote operations. The round is jointly led by Swedish defence VC Gungnir Capital and Danish PSV Hafnium with participation from EIFO, SAP9 Group and GreenUP IV Invest. “This round gives us […] The post Unmanned cargo aircraft developer Acodyne secures €2.5 million for defence and offshore logistics appeared first on EU-Startups .

companies.name: Modelence
companies.blurb: How do you build tooling for the AI software stack? A company called Modelence has an interesting answer.

companies.name: VC firm 2150
companies.blurb: Europe-based venture fund 2150 raised the money to invest in climate tech startups that help solve cities' carbon footprint.

companies.name: A new search engine
companies.blurb: Lore raises $1.1 million to build a personalized search engine for fandom culture.

companies.name: AI voice company Hyper
companies.blurb: Hyper hopes to use the fresh capital to help scale across the country, integrate more into existing 911 systems, hire a head of engineering, and build its next product.

companies.name: Applied Intuition
companies.blurb: The buzzy AI-powered autonomous vehicle software company doubles its valuation from last year -- and then some.

companies.name: British asset finance platform equipal
companies.blurb: equipal, a FinTech funder of business equipment out of London, has secured €18.84 million (£16.25 million) in a combined investment in order to hire across business development, marketing, operations, credit and data where the team expects to grow to 12 in the next year. The funding includes a €1.4 million (£1.25 million) equity investment alongside […] The post British asset finance platform equipal secures €18.84 million to grow team and lending capacity appeared first on EU-Startups .

companies.name: Orbio
companies.blurb: Orbio announces $21 million Series A in round led by Dawn Capital.

companies.name: Benchmark
companies.blurb: The legendary Silicon Valley VC firm abandons its more than 20-year tradition of keeping its funds to about $425 million.

companies.name: Superorganism
companies.blurb: Superorganism backs companies across three categories: tech that slows or reverses extinction, startups that operate at the intersection of climate and biodiversity, and tools that enable conservationists to do their work more effectively.

companies.name: A year after filing to IPO, still-private Cerebras Systems
companies.blurb: Cerebras initially filed to go public in September 2024 but ran into several regulatory delays due to foreign investments.

companies.name: Doorstep
companies.blurb: Doorstep integrates into existing delivery apps and then, using phone sensors, tracks when a driver has entered a building, gone up an elevator, and made it to the desired doorstep.
